perturbation.original_text: This Agreement will be governed by the laws of the State of New York and will be binding upon and inure to the benefit of the parties hereto and their respective successors and assigns.
perturbation.changed_text: Neither party hereto makes any representation or warranty, express or implied, on which the other party may rely as to the accuracy or completeness of the Proprietary Information and only those representations and warranties made in writing in a definitive agreement for a Possible Transaction, if any, shall 4 have any legal effect. This Agreement will be governed by the laws of the State of New York and will be binding upon and inure to the benefit of the parties hereto and their respective successors and assigns.
perturbation.explanation: By embedding the governing law clause within a disclaimer about representations and warranties, its prominence and enforceability are reduced. This is because it obscures the clarity that the contract is explicitly to be interpreted under the laws of New York. It implies that the importance of this is less than the accuracy of provided information.
perturbation.law_explanation: NY Gen. Oblig. Law § 5-1401 allows parties to a contract of $250,000 or more to choose New York law to govern the agreement. By placing this clause within a section disclaiming warranties, its intended legal effect and enforceability are undermined, potentially creating ambiguity as to whether the parties genuinely intended for New York law to govern all aspects of the agreement, especially if disputes arise related to the accuracy of information shared. Courts look at if there was specific and mutual assent to be governed by the contract.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: NYS Open Legislation | NYSenate.gov Sorry, you need to enable JavaScript to visit this website. Specifying a milestone date will retrieve the most recent version of the location before that date. See most recent version before or on: 2014-09-22 2015-10-09 2017-10-27 2018-04-20 2018-07-06 2018-07-13 2018-08-31 2019-01-11 2019-01-18 2019-06-28 2019-07-05 2019-07-19 2019-08-16 2019-10-18 2019-11-22 2019-12-13 2020-02-21 2020-04-10 2020-12-18 2021-01-08 2021-04-02 2021-04-09 2021-06-18 2021-09-24 2021-12-31 2022-02-25 2022-03-04 2022-12-30 2023-01-06 2023-10-27 2023-11-26 2024-12-20 2025-01-03 Share Facebook Email SECTION 5-1401 Choice of law General Obligations (GOB) CHAPTER 24-A, ARTICLE 5, TITLE 14 § 5-1401. Choice of law. 1. The parties to any contract, agreement or undertaking, contingent or otherwise, in consideration of, or relating to any obligation arising out of a transaction covering in the aggregate not less than two hundred fifty thousand dollars, including a transaction otherwise covered by subsection (a) of section 1--301 of the uniform commercial code, may agree that the law of this state shall govern their rights and duties in whole or in part, whether or not such contract, agreement or undertaking bears a reasonable relation to this state. This section shall not apply to any contract, agreement or undertaking (a) for labor or personal services, (b) relating to any transaction for personal, family or household services, or (c) to the extent provided to the contrary in subsection (c) of section 1--301 of the uniform commercial code. 2. Nothing contained in this section shall be construed to limit or deny the enforcement of any provision respecting choice of law in any other contract, agreement or undertaking.
perturbation.justification: The modification introduces a structural flaw by embedding the governing law clause within a disclaimer about representations and warranties, which undermines its prominence and enforceability. This contradicts the intent of NY Gen. Oblig. Law § 5-1401, as explained in the Law Explanation, which aims to ensure that parties' choice of New York law is clear and binding. Placing it within a disclaimer creates ambiguity as to whether the parties genuinely intended for New York law to govern all aspects of the agreement. Scraped Law Snippet 1 supports this by stating that the parties to a contract may agree that the law of this state shall govern their rights and duties. The modification obscures the explicit agreement and could be interpreted to mean that governing law is secondary to the disclaimer, thus a contradiction exists.

perturbation.original_text: It is understood and agreed that money damages would not be a sufficient remedy for any breach of this Agreement and that each party shall be entitled to specific performance as a remedy for any such breach or threatened breach of this Agreement. This remedy shall not be deemed to be the exclusive remedy for any breach or threatened breach of this Agreement but shall be in addition to all other remedies available at law or equity.
perturbation.changed_text: The term “ Representative ” means, as to any person, such person’s affiliates, officers, directors, general partners, members, employees, consultants, investment bankers, financial advisors, accountants, legal counsel, consultants and potential lenders. It is understood and agreed that money damages would not be a sufficient remedy for any breach of this Agreement and that each party shall be entitled to specific performance as a remedy for any such breach or threatened breach of this Agreement. This remedy shall not be deemed to be the exclusive remedy for any breach or threatened breach of this Agreement but shall be in addition to all other remedies available at law or equity.
perturbation.explanation: Moving the 'specific performance' clause into the 'Representative' definition section buries the important remedy provision within definitional text. This makes it harder to find and more easily overlooked, weakening its enforceability. It is difficult to locate remedies for breaches of contract.
perturbation.contradicted_law: New York Civil Practice Law and Rules (CPLR) § 7503
perturbation.law_citation: New York Civil Practice Law and Rules (CPLR) § 7503
perturbation.law_explanation: CPLR § 7503 governs applications to compel or stay arbitration in New York. Burying the specific performance clause could affect a court's determination of whether the parties clearly and unequivocally agreed to specific performance as a remedy. It can be argued it was not reasonably conspicuous.

Application to compel or stay arbitration; stay of action; notice of intention to arbitrate. (a) Application to compel arbitration; stay of action. A party aggrieved by the failure of another to arbitrate may apply for an order compelling arbitration. Where there is no substantial question whether a valid agreement was made or complied with, and the claim sought to be arbitrated is not barred by limitation under subdivision (b) of section 7502, the court shall direct the parties to arbitrate. Where any such question is raised, it shall be tried forthwith in said court. If an issue claimed to be arbitrable is involved in an action pending in a court having jurisdiction to hear a motion to compel arbitration, the application shall be made by motion in that action. If the application is granted, the order shall operate to stay a pending or subsequent action, or so much of it as is referable to arbitration. (b) Application to stay arbitration. Subject to the provisions of subdivision (c), a party who has not participated in the arbitration and who has not made or been served with an application to compel arbitration, may apply to stay arbitration on the ground that a valid agreement was not made or has not been complied with or that the claim sought to be arbitrated is barred by limitation under subdivision (b) of section 7502. (c) Notice of intention to arbitrate. A party may serve upon another party a demand for arbitration or a notice of intention to arbitrate, specifying the agreement pursuant to which arbitration is sought and the name and address of the party serving the notice, or of an officer or agent thereof if such party is an association or corporation, and stating that unless the party served applies to stay the arbitration within twenty days after such service he shall thereafter be precluded from objec

perturbation.justification: The structural flaw of burying the 'specific performance' clause within a definitional section weakens its enforceability, potentially affecting a court's determination of whether the parties clearly agreed to it. The LLM explanation references CPLR § 7503, governing applications to compel or stay arbitration, indicating that the conspicuousness of such clauses is relevant. While the provided snippets of CPLR § 7503 do not explicitly mention 'conspicuousness', the essence of the law is ensuring a 'valid agreement was made'. Making a key remedy provision difficult to find argues against a valid, knowing agreement, thus creating a contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: This remedy shall not be deemed to be the exclusive remedy for any breach or threatened breach of this Agreement but shall be in addition to all other remedies available at law or equity.
perturbation.changed_text: The term “ affiliate ” has the meaning set forth in Rule 12b-2 of the regulations promulgated under Securities Exchange Act of 1934, as amended (the “ Exchange Act ”). This remedy shall not be deemed to be the exclusive remedy for any breach or threatened breach of this Agreement but shall be in addition to all other remedies available at law or equity.
perturbation.explanation: Moving the statement that 'remedies available are not exclusive' to the definition of affiliate section weakens its impact. This clause is critical as it clarifies that the party can pursue further legal actions and remedies outside of those explicitly outlined in the contract, and is typically located in a section discussing the implications if terms are broken. Putting it in the affiliate section gives less prominence to that clause.
perturbation.contradicted_law: New York common law principles of contract interpretation
perturbation.law_citation: New York common law principles of contract interpretation
perturbation.law_explanation: Under New York common law, contract interpretation emphasizes giving effect to the parties' intent as expressed in the contract language. The placement suggests it is related only to the defined term. Furthermore, remedies are diluted and hidden away in definitional sections, and the other party can try to make arguments to confuse a court.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a structural flaw by moving a critical clause regarding the non-exclusive nature of remedies to the definition of 'affiliate,' weakening its impact. This contradicts the principle of giving effect to the parties' intent as expressed in the contract language, as highlighted in the Law Explanation. While Scraped Law Snippet 1 is empty, Scraped Law Snippet 2 contains irrelevant information, but based on the Perturbation Explanation and Law Explanation, placing the remedies clause in an irrelevant definitional section reduces its prominence and could confuse a court, creating a structural flaw that impacts enforceability and clarity of the contract.

perturbation.original_text: The parties hereto agree that unless and until a definitive agreement is executed and delivered with respect to the Possible Transaction, neither party intends to be, nor shall either be, under any legal obligation with respect to the Possible Transaction or otherwise, by virtue of any written or oral expressions by themselves or their respective Representatives with respect to the Possible Transaction, including any obligation to commence or continue discussions or negotiations, except for the matters specifically agreed to in this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: In consideration of being furnished the Proprietary Information by the other party, each party agrees that, for a period of twelve (12) months from the date of this Agreement, such party and its Restricted Affiliates shall not employ or solicit to employ any of the current officers or employees of the other party or its subsidiaries with whom such party or its Representatives has had contact in connection with the evaluation of the Possible Transaction or who were specifically identified to such party during the period of investigation of the other party pursuant to this Agreement, without obtaining the prior written consent of the other party. The parties hereto agree that unless and until a definitive agreement is executed and delivered with respect to the Possible Transaction, neither party intends to be, nor shall either be, under any legal obligation with respect to the Possible Transaction or otherwise, by virtue of any written or oral expressions by themselves or their respective Representatives with respect to the Possible Transaction, including any obligation to commence or continue discussions or negotiations, except for the matters specifically agreed to in this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: By relocating the "no legal obligation until definitive agreement" clause into the non-solicitation section, the enforceability of the clause is reduced. This clause limits preliminary liability, but by relocating it to the non-solicitation section, the legal effect of this clause is reduced. Now, it appears that the limitation of liability is linked with the non-solicitation agreement, which is incorrect and can be seen as misleading.
perturbation.contradicted_law: New York law on preliminary agreements and letters of intent
perturbation.law_citation: IDT Corp. v. Tyco Group, S.A.R.L., 13 N.Y.3d 209 (2009)
perturbation.law_explanation: New York courts require a clear expression of intent not to be bound absent a definitive agreement. Shifting it to the non-solicitation clause suggests an intent to link this, reducing the prominence of this clause.
perturbation.justification: The perturbation involves a 'Structural Flaws - Legal Contradiction,' where the clause negating legal obligations until a definitive agreement is reached is moved into the non-solicitation section. This relocation reduces the prominence and enforceability of the 'no legal obligation' clause. The Law Explanation emphasizes that New York courts require a clear expression of intent not to be bound absent a definitive agreement. Relocating the clause reduces its prominence and creates ambiguity about its scope. Since the definition of Structural Flaws Legal Contradiction states that this type of contradiction arises when a required legal disclosure is placed in an irrelevant or misleading section making it difficult for parties to locate or interpret, this applies here.
